mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is near the effective lower bound, equity duration dominates and small rate declines reduce discount rates faster than earnings expectations weaken, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of periods with FEDFUNDS below 1% and Rank IC stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals deteriorating labor income growth and weaker household spending, which should pressure cyclicals and the broad index lower over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time or if the average forward return is not negative at the 5% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: When household spending and retail sales both accelerate, revenue growth expectations improve and equities should rally over the next week, with the effect strongest when both measures move together rather than separately.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-signal episodes do not outperform single-series episodes, or if 5-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of instances where both RSAFS and GDPC1 are rising.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO reflects improving industrial output and near-term earnings leverage, which should lift the S&P 500 over the following month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises month-over-month for 2 consecutive releases
failure_condition: Reject if month-over-month INDPRO increases are followed by negative or flat 20-day SPX returns on average, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 across any 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts are a leading demand barometer; a sharp rise indicates stronger construction activity, wealth effects, and downstream consumption, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 10% year-over-year
failure_condition: Reject if above-threshold housing start expansions fail to produce positive 60-day forward SPX returns more than 55% of the time or if the mean forward return is not greater than zero.

mechanism_narrative: If CPI inflation is decelerating while policy rates remain unchanged, real rates rise less than expected and the Fed retains room to stay accommodative, which should be supportive for equities over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation over 3 months falls while FEDFUNDS is unchanged
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation-plus-unchanged-policy episodes do not deliver positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the joint effect is indistinguishable from CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: During a labor-market shock, falling PAYEMS combined with rising UNRATE signals shrinking payroll income and higher recession odds, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month while UNRATE rises month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if the joint deterioration signal is followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 40% of cases or if the conditional mean return is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a faster rise in FEDFUNDS than CPIAUCSL raises real borrowing costs, compresses valuation multiples, and should depress S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if tightening episodes with rising rate-pressure produce positive 60-day SPX returns more than 45% of the time, or if the effect disappears once CPIAUCSL is controlled for.
